title: Add-Content without -Encoding creates files in ANSI (system default) encoding
description: In PowerShell 5.1, Add-Content without -Encoding uses the system default encoding (typically Windows-1252 ANSI) when creating new files. While it preserves existing file encoding when appending, new files lose Unicode characters. For consistent encoding across Set-Content, Out-File, and Add-Content, always specify -Encoding UTF8. This is advisory because Add-Content on existing UTF-8 files preserves encoding, making it less severe than Out-File or Set-Content without encoding.
detection_method: Regex scan for Add-Content calls missing -Encoding parameter
preventionRule: Always specify -Encoding UTF8 on Add-Content to ensure consistent encoding, especially when files may not exist yet.
